criteria.name: Review cross-check completed
criteria.evidence: The claimed implementation files exist under the untracked `src/tools/video-editor/` tree, which is why `git status` reports the directory rather than each file. The execution-audit file-level mismatch is advisory, not a contradiction of the executor evidence.

criteria.name: Moving a clip on the preview canvas translates it without changing its width or height
criteria.evidence: `OverlayEditor.tsx:327-387` correctly separates move from resize, so the shape-distortion bug is fixed. But drag listeners are only installed by the effect at `OverlayEditor.tsx:311-408`, while `startDrag` at `OverlayEditor.tsx:410-431` only mutates a ref and calls `onSelectClip`. Because `PreviewPanel.tsx:84-95` passes raw `setSelectedClipId`, dragging an already-selected overlay may not rerender, so the move interaction can fail to start.

criteria.name: Cursor movement maps 1:1 to clip movement regardless of preview zoom level
criteria.evidence: `OverlayEditor.tsx:322-323` applies stored `scaleX/scaleY`, and `OverlayEditor.tsx:415-423` captures those scales from layout at drag start. The math is correct, but the same drag-start lifecycle bug means the interaction is not reliably active on repeated drags, so the criterion is not fully met.

criteria.name: Corner resize handles change clip dimensions as expected
criteria.evidence: Corner resize math is present at `OverlayEditor.tsx:373-386`, but it runs only after the same listener-install path at `OverlayEditor.tsx:311-408`. Repeated resize attempts on an already-selected clip can no-op because no rerender is forced in `startDrag`.

criteria.name: Edge handles crop the clip from the corresponding side
criteria.evidence: Crop modes, handle rendering, and clamped crop math are implemented at `OverlayEditor.tsx:23-32`, `OverlayEditor.tsx:330-371`, and `OverlayEditor.tsx:601-632`, with render support in `VisualClip.tsx:44-50`. But crop drags share the same unreliable listener setup, so edge-crop interaction is not dependable.

criteria.name: No ghost/shadow DOM elements linger after any drag operation on the timeline
criteria.evidence: The old origin ghost has been removed: there is no remaining `dragOriginStyle` path, and the only extra preview layer during drag is the active overlay preview at `OverlayEditor.tsx:498-530`. The selected-crop ghost at `OverlayEditor.tsx:485-545` is intentional and only appears for cropped selected clips, matching the approved design.

criteria.name: Rapid drag sequences and edge cases (blur, Escape, drop-outside) leave no orphaned DOM nodes
criteria.evidence: `OverlayEditor.tsx:311-408` only wires `mousemove` and `mouseup`. There is no `blur`, `pointercancel`, `mouseleave`, or `Escape` cancellation path, so interrupted drags can leave `dragState`/`dragOverride` active and keep the live drag preview rendered.

criteria.name: Existing overlay interactions (text editing, double-click, selection) remain functional
criteria.evidence: Text editing and double-click handling are still present at `OverlayEditor.tsx:433-458` and `OverlayEditor.tsx:570-575`, and selection still routes through `onSelectClip` at `OverlayEditor.tsx:577-580`.

criteria.name: DECISION-001 implemented correctly
criteria.evidence: The resize-direction checks are now inside the non-move branch at `OverlayEditor.tsx:327-387`, so `'move'.includes('e')` no longer mutates width/height during move.

criteria.name: DECISION-002 implemented correctly
criteria.evidence: The old drag-origin ghost is gone, and the concept was repurposed into the selected cropped-bounds ghost at `OverlayEditor.tsx:485-545`.

criteria.name: DECISION-003 implemented correctly
criteria.evidence: Crop is rendered via CSS `clipPath: inset(...)` in `VisualClip.tsx:44-50`, not via a rendering-pipeline restructure.

criteria.name: DECISION-004 respected
criteria.evidence: The execution summary batches follow the approved sequence: T1, then T2/T3, then T4, then T5/T7, then T6, then T8.

criteria.name: DECISION-005 implemented correctly
criteria.evidence: Edge crop handles are gated behind `!overlay.isText` at `OverlayEditor.tsx:601-632`, so text clips do not expose crop handles.

issues: `OverlayEditor.tsx:311-431` still has a drag lifecycle bug. `startDrag` only mutates `dragState.current` and relies on a rerender to make the effect attach `mousemove`/`mouseup` listeners. Because `PreviewPanel.tsx:94` passes raw `setSelectedClipId`, dragging an already-selected overlay can fail entirely. Move, resize, and crop all inherit this.

`OverlayEditor.tsx:311-408` does not clean up interrupted drags on `window.blur`, `Escape`, or other cancellation paths. That leaves the live drag preview/override vulnerable to lingering after aborted interactions, so the edge-case cleanup criterion is still unmet.
summary: Needs rework. The executor implemented the approved shape-preserving move fix, crop model, serialization round-trip, selected-crop ghost, and Remotion `clip-path` rendering, and the settled decisions were followed. But `OverlayEditor` still relies on a rerender to attach drag listeners, so repeated drags on an already-selected clip can fail, and interrupted drags still lack blur/Escape cleanup. That leaves the core interaction criteria only partially satisfied.

task_verdicts.reviewer_verdict: Pass. The move-vs-resize string-matching bug is fixed by the guarded non-move branch in `OverlayEditor.tsx`.
Needs rework. The coordinate scaling is implemented, but the drag lifecycle is still unreliable because listener registration depends on a rerender after `startDrag`.
Pass. The old drag-origin ghost rendering was removed, matching the approved decision.
Pass. Crop fields were added to the type/model, both timeline-data mapping directions, the serialize whitelist, and reset-position handling.
Needs rework. Edge crop handles, crop math, and crop-aware bounds are present, but the same drag-start bug prevents reliable crop interaction and there is still no interrupted-drag cleanup.
Pass. The selected cropped clip now shows a full-bounds ghost behind the visible cropped area.
Pass. Crop is wired into Remotion rendering via `clipPath: inset(top right bottom left)`.
Needs rework. The validation missed the drag-listener lifecycle bug and the absence of blur/Escape cleanup, so the success criteria were overstated.

sense_check_verdicts.verdict: Confirmed. The move branch at `OverlayEditor.tsx:327-329` only updates `x` and `y`, and the resize-direction checks are gated behind the `else` branch.

Not fully confirmed. The scaling math is correct, but the interaction is not reliable because listeners are only attached after a rerender, which may not happen when dragging an already-selected clip.
Confirmed for the original artifact. The dashed origin ghost was removed; the remaining selected-crop ghost is intentional and separately gated.
Confirmed. Crop fields are present in `TimelineClip`, `ClipMeta`, `getDefaultClipMeta`, `rowsToConfig`, `TIMELINE_CLIP_FIELDS`, and reset-position handling.
Partially confirmed. The handles are non-text-only and the crop clamp math is correct, but reliable edge-crop interaction is undermined by the same drag-listener registration bug.
Confirmed. The full-bounds ghost is rendered only when the overlay is selected and has non-zero crop values.
Confirmed. `VisualClip.tsx` applies `clipPath = inset(top right bottom left)`, so `cropLeft` maps to the fourth inset argument as expected.
Not confirmed. Text editing, selection, and serialization paths remain wired, but repeated drag interactions and interrupted-drag cleanup are still not robust, and no live UI validation was run.
